element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns stealth is required; harming civilians triggers mission failure via arrest
Different characters have different skills; player must learn which to use for progression
Detection by guards triggers security response; discretion advised
High damage output; requires learning avoidance patterns
Detection brings guards; stealth and evasion required
Vertical navigation adds complexity; coordination between levels

element_introduction_order.element: Boss encounters (Slash Stiletto - grey variant with bombs)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Introduces pattern-based combat with projectile hazards
High melee damage; static positioning requires approach planning
Increased mobility and damage vs stationary variants
Ranged threat; requires dodging and cover awareness
High damage projectiles; evasion and spacing critical
Sustained damage hazard; requires tactical avoidance

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching safe street areas between infiltrations
Successfully switching to appropriate character for puzzle/combat section
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Alternating stealth/infiltration sequences (tension) with street traversal and character management (relief); damage values escalate significantly in late stages

difficulty_curve.early_game: Street-level introduction emphasizes non-lethal stealth rules; basic enemy types and single-floor navigation
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Multi-floor installations introduce vertical complexity; diverse enemy types with ranged and melee attacks; environmental hazards multiply
difficulty_curve.late_game: Final stage concentrates high-damage enemies (Jorgé Burns flamethrower); boss encounters with complex patterns; Swiss Alps setting
notable_spikes: Stage 1 end boss (Slash Stiletto grey variant with bombs)
Stage 2 knight sequences (high melee damage, especially walking variants)
Final stage concentration of flamethrower enemies and complex multi-floor layouts

notable_level_patterns.description: Opening areas where civilians present and player must avoid harming them; arrest mechanic teaches stealth philosophy before combat
Enemy hideout sequences requiring character skill switching and surveillance avoidance; discretion and planning emphasized
Vertical puzzle structures requiring traversal across multiple levels; coordination and hazard awareness necessary
Pattern-based combat requiring dodge timing and positioning; first appears at Stage 1 conclusion
Areas with high-damage static enemies (knights Stage 2) requiring careful approach planning and character selection

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Mission: Impossible (NES)
